questions.q: Chandragupta I is regarded as the ruler who raised which family to imperial status?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I elevated the Gupta family to imperial rank in the early 4th century CE.

questions.q: In which century CE did Chandragupta I establish Gupta imperial power?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I came to power in the early 4th century CE, conventionally around 320 CE.

questions.q: Chandragupta I strengthened his position through a marriage alliance with which people?
questions.explanation: His marriage to a Lichchhavi princess greatly boosted the prestige and power of the early Guptas.

questions.q: Which grand title did Chandragupta I adopt?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I took the title Maharajadhiraja, 'great king of kings', marking imperial status.

questions.q: Chandragupta I was the founder-figure of the golden age associated with which empire?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I laid the foundation for the Gupta Empire's celebrated classical age.

questions.q: Who succeeded Chandragupta I as Gupta emperor?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I was succeeded by his son Samudragupta, the great conqueror.

questions.q: The Gupta heartland under Chandragupta I lay in which broad region?
questions.explanation: The early Gupta domain was centred in northern India, in the Magadha region of present-day Bihar.

questions.q: Chandragupta I should NOT be confused with which earlier founder of a different empire?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I of the Guptas is distinct from Chandragupta Maurya, who founded the Mauryan Empire centuries earlier.

questions.q: The princess Chandragupta I married was named?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I married the Lichchhavi princess Kumaradevi, a union commemorated on coins.

questions.q: Chandragupta I belonged to which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I was an early ruler of the Gupta dynasty, which he raised to empire.

questions.q: A special coin type commemorating the union of Chandragupta I and his queen is known as the?
questions.explanation: The 'King and Queen' gold coins jointly name Chandragupta I and Kumaradevi, underscoring the Lichchhavi alliance.

questions.q: The Lichchhavis, into whom Chandragupta I married, were an old people associated with which ancient republic?
questions.explanation: The Lichchhavis were a celebrated gana-sangha (clan-republic) centred on Vaishali, lending the Guptas prestige.

questions.q: Many scholars associate the start of the Gupta era (a dating system) with the accession of which ruler around 319/320 CE?
questions.explanation: The Gupta calendar era is usually reckoned from about 319/320 CE, linked to Chandragupta I's accession.

questions.q: Who is named as the predecessor of Chandragupta I in the Gupta genealogy?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I's father was Ghatotkacha, who, like Sri Gupta, held the lesser title Maharaja.

questions.q: The Allahabad pillar inscription, though mainly about Samudragupta, helps establish the genealogy of which earlier ruler?
questions.explanation: The Prayag Prashasti traces the Gupta lineage, confirming Chandragupta I as Samudragupta's father and an imperial ruler.

questions.q: Compared with his father Ghatotkacha, Chandragupta I's adoption of Maharajadhiraja signalled what?
options: A leap from local king to paramount emperor
questions.explanation: Where his predecessors used the modest 'Maharaja', Chandragupta I's 'Maharajadhiraja' announced paramount sovereignty.

questions.q: The marriage alliance with the Lichchhavis is widely thought to have helped the Guptas gain control over which key region?
questions.explanation: The Lichchhavi connection is generally seen as strengthening Gupta authority over Magadha and the Ganges heartland.

questions.q: On the King-Queen coins, the reverse often shows a seated goddess associated with what?
questions.explanation: The reverse of the King-Queen type typically depicts a seated goddess on a lion, an auspicious royal motif.

questions.q: Chandragupta I's reign is generally placed in which span of years?
questions.explanation: Chandragupta I is conventionally dated to roughly 320-335 CE, after which Samudragupta succeeded.

questions.q: What does the term 'Maharajadhiraja' literally convey about Chandragupta I's claimed rank?
questions.explanation: Maharajadhiraja means 'great king of kings', asserting overlordship over subordinate rulers.

questions.q: The inclusion of Queen Kumaradevi's name and image on imperial coinage was significant because it was?
options: A demand from the Roman Senate
An unusual public acknowledgement of the alliance
questions.explanation: Naming and depicting the queen on coins was a notable way to advertise the politically vital Lichchhavi alliance.

questions.q: Chandragupta I's capital is generally identified with which ancient city?
questions.explanation: Pataliputra in Magadha is generally regarded as the principal seat of the early Guptas under Chandragupta I.

questions.q: The phrase 'Lichchhavi-dauhitra' applied in Gupta records to Samudragupta highlights his descent through which line?
questions.explanation: Samudragupta is proudly styled 'Lichchhavi-dauhitra' (daughter's son of the Lichchhavis), underlining the prestige of Chandragupta I's marriage.

questions.q: Why is the exact extent of Chandragupta I's own conquests difficult to determine?
options: He left thousands of detailed maps
Sources focus on his successor and his marriage alliance more than his campaigns
He minted no coins at all
questions.explanation: Much of what is known centres on the Lichchhavi alliance and Samudragupta's record, leaving Chandragupta I's direct conquests less clear.

questions.q: Some historians debate whether the Gupta era of 319/320 CE marks Chandragupta I's coronation or what alternative?
options: The formal foundation/accession of the dynasty's imperial phase
The building of the Taj Mahal
questions.explanation: Scholars dispute whether the era marks his coronation precisely or the broader imperial foundation, but it is tied to his accession.

questions.q: The two pre-imperial Gupta rulers preceding Chandragupta I, who bore only the title Maharaja, were?
questions.explanation: Sri Gupta and Ghatotkacha, styled only Maharaja, preceded Chandragupta I, who first claimed Maharajadhiraja.

questions.q: The numismatic evidence linking Chandragupta I and Kumaradevi is important partly because it predates which later Gupta coin innovations?
options: The varied figural gold types of Samudragupta and Chandragupta II
questions.explanation: The King-Queen issue is among the earliest Gupta gold coins, preceding the elaborate figural types of later Gupta rulers.

questions.q: Chandragupta I's strategy of consolidating Magadha set the stage for which immediate imperial expansion?
options: The Chola naval expedition to Srivijaya
questions.explanation: By securing the Magadhan core, Chandragupta I provided the base from which Samudragupta launched his wide conquests.

questions.q: The Lichchhavis had earlier been a republican power; their late absorption into Gupta fortunes illustrates what political trend?
options: The spread of democracy across India
The decline of old republics and rise of imperial monarchy
The triumph of maritime trade leagues
questions.explanation: The Gupta-Lichchhavi link reflects the eclipse of older clan-republics by the centralising imperial monarchy of the Guptas.

questions.q: Within the Gupta genealogy, Chandragupta I is best described as which generation of the recorded line?
options: The first ancestor of the family
The third named ruler, after Sri Gupta and Ghatotkacha
questions.explanation: In the Gupta records Chandragupta I is the third named ruler, the first to assume imperial titles after Sri Gupta and Ghatotkacha.
